content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95814 • • • 1102 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance Administration . . . Executive, Legal . . . Enforcement Center of State
(916) 322-4800           322-4921           322-4811

May 5, 1976

Honorable Joseph B. Montoya
Assemblyman, Sixtieth District
State Capitol
Sacramento CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 76-059

Dear Assemblyman Montoya:

Thank you for your letter of April 23, 1976, requesting an opinion concerning the interplay of Health and Safety Code section 33385.5, proposed AB 3673 which would amend that section, and Government Code Section 87100 prohibiting conflicts of interest by public officials. Because of the urgency of your request, I instructed Kenneth Coshorn, an attorney in the Conflict of Interest division, to contact Mr. Jeff Loeb of your staff concerning this matter. This has been accomplished, and this letter will serve as a confirmation of those discussions.

The general question you ask (does a project area committee, as constituted, violate the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act?) has been the subject of extensive discussion within the Commission staff, and an opinion request by Mr. Don Bonfa, City Attorney, City of Huntington Beach, asking precisely the same question, has been presented. As Mr. Coshorn informed Mr. Loeb, a first hearing on a staff draft of the opinion requested by Mr. Bonfa will be held Thursday, May 6. I understand a representative from your staff has received a copy of that draft and will be at that hearing. We will keep your office informed of any developments concerning the opinion, and the date, time and place for a second hearing, which probably will be held in June. For these reasons, the Commission will not render a formal opinion in response to your request since it will undoubtedly be resolved by the pending opinion request.

As you may have observed, the tentative proposed conclusion in the Bonfa opinion is that PAC members are "public officials" within the meaning of Government Code Section 87100, but their participation is not barred by Section 87100 except when the PAC's decision would have a particular effect on the individual's financial interest as distinguished from its

116

A
Hon Joseph B. Montoya
May 5, 1978
Page Two


effect on the interests of the general class of persons of
which he is a part.

In light of the draft's primary conclusion that even
under existing law PAC members are "public officials," I do
not believe the proposed amendment, strengthening the powers
of the PAC, would affect the outcome of the bending opinion
request. While it would reinforce a Commission conclusion that
PAC members are covered by Section 87100, it would not, under
the proposed draft, negatively affect their ability to partici-
pate in the general manner described above.

I trust this answers your questions. If, however, you
want to appeal the denial of your opinion request, you may
do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. Also, please
feel free to call upon Mr. Goshorn at any time to keep your
office abreast of developments concerning the pending opinion.
Thank you for your interest in this very important question.

Sincerely,


Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:pvp

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
2319 K STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA
95814
SACRAMENTO

Assembling
California Legislature

[stamped circle with date]

Date Received 4-26-'76
Response Due 5-10-76

MEMBERS
CALIFORNIA
ADVISORY COUNCIL
ON EDUCATION AND
TECHNICAL TRAINING
STATE SENATORS A-D
REGENTS ADVISORY BOARD

JOSEPH B. MONTOYA
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTIETH DISTRICT
BAGGETT, BALDWIN PARK, CITY OF INDUSTRY, EL MONTE,
LA FUENTE, NORTH WHITTIER, ROSEHEAD,
SOUTH EL MONTE, SOUTH SAN GABRIEL,
WEST COVINA, VALINDA

MAJORITY WHIP

April 23, 1976

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 "J" Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

This letter is to request an opinion on the following
question of law:

Under the provisions of proposed AB 3673 (Section 33385.5)
a Project Area Committee (PAC) is constituted with designated
powers of review and comment. Among those powers granted is the
authority to disapprove a proposed redevelopment plan. The effect
of such disapproval will be to require that in order to
proceed with the plan the local legislative body must approve
by a 2/3 majority.

The problem that may arise comes under Section 87100 et
seq. of the Political Reform Act of 1974. The Project Area
Committee will be composed of residents of the affected areas--
businesspersons, domiciliaries, etc.--all of whom may (and
probably will) have a financial interest in the outcome of the
vote of the PAC.

The questions thus is twofold:

(1) Does the PAC, as constituted under Section 33385.5
violate the Conflicts of Interest provision of the
Political Reform Act?

(2) Is there an alternate method of guaranteeing necessary citizen control by the PAC without such violation?

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein                -2-                April 23, 1976




     Our intent in this is to structure a viable method of
insuring citizen input into the Community Redevelopment Pro-
cess. We would appreciate your comments by April 30 in order
to facilitate drafting the appropriate language for our next
hearing.


                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        JOSEPH B. MONTOYA


JBM:ltb

Attachment
